selection_policy: OpenAlex topic harvesting across breakthrough families and technology corridors.
Strict age-adjusted citation thresholds to ensure only high-impact papers enter the atlas.
Priority for top-tier venues, landmark topic match, cross-domain breadth, and citation depth.
Primary focus on 1995-present engineering and STEM literature with DOI-backed scholarly records.
Manual anchors for Nobel-calibre and foundational papers that define what 'epochal' means.

categories.label: New observability
categories.description: Papers that make hidden phenomena measurable, imageable, traceable, or monitorable.

categories.label: New instrument or platform
categories.description: Papers that create reusable experimental, computational, or operational platforms.

categories.label: Representation and modeling shift
categories.description: Papers that change the representation so the problem becomes structurally easier.

categories.label: Search, optimization, and control
categories.description: Papers that reduce discovery cost, improve control, or create adaptive decision loops.

categories.label: Shared infrastructure and protocols
categories.description: Papers that create shared abstractions, datasets, protocols, or system architectures.

categories.label: New substrate, material, or device
categories.description: Papers that open a new physical or programmable platform for downstream engineering.

categories.label: Fabrication, synthesis, and process route
categories.description: Papers that make promising phenomena repeatable, manufacturable, or scalable.

categories.label: Scaling architecture
categories.description: Papers that explain how capability survives larger systems, higher throughput, or tighter constraints.

categories.label: Validation, trust, and reproducibility
categories.description: Papers that make claims more reliable, testable, reproducible, or transferable.

categories.label: Cross-domain transfer
categories.description: Papers whose methods or platforms later became productive well beyond the original domain.

corridors.label: AI and compute
corridors.description: Learning systems, foundation models, computer vision, scientific AI, and compute abstractions.

corridors.label: Systems and networks
corridors.description: Distributed systems, cloud infrastructure, datacenter protocols, and software platforms.

corridors.label: Semiconductors and electronics
corridors.description: Transistors, memories, lithography, chip architectures, and electronic device platforms.

corridors.label: Photonics, imaging, and sensing
corridors.description: Optics, imaging, metrology, microscopy, sensing, and photonic systems.

corridors.label: Robotics, autonomy, and control
corridors.description: Robotic manipulation, autonomy, control theory, perception, and embodied systems.

corridors.label: Materials, chemistry, and energy
corridors.description: Materials platforms, energy devices, catalysis, chemical systems, and discovery infrastructure.

corridors.label: Biotech and programmable biology
corridors.description: Genome editing, sequencing, synthetic biology, protein engineering, and biological programmability.

corridors.label: Quantum and precision technology
corridors.description: Quantum information, qubit platforms, precision metrology, and sensing.

corridors.label: Manufacturing and cyber-physical systems
corridors.description: Industrial process control, digital twins, self-driving labs, and operational intelligence.

corridors.label: Climate, Earth, space, and scientific computing
corridors.description: Climate systems, Earth observation, scientific simulation, and space technologies.

sources.title: Observation of a new boson at a mass of 125 GeV with the CMS experiment at the LHC
sources.authors: CMS Collaboration
sources.note: Discovery of the Higgs boson at the LHC. (Englert & Higgs: Nobel Physics 2013)

sources.title: Learning representations by back-propagating errors
sources.authors: David E. Rumelhart, Geoffrey E. Hinton, Ronald J. Williams
sources.note: Introduced backpropagation for training multi-layer neural networks. The foundational algorithm of all deep learning. (Hinton: Nobel Physics 2024)

sources.title: Deep Residual Learning for Image Recognition
sources.venue: 2016 IEEE Conference on Computer Vision and Pattern Recognition (CVPR)
sources.authors: Kaiming He, Xiangyu Zhang, Shaoqing Ren, Jian Sun
matched_topics: deep residual learning for image recognition
sources.note: Introduced residual connections (skip connections), enabling training of 100+ layer networks. ResNet won ILSVRC 2015 and became a universal backbone.

sources.title: Attention Is All You Need
sources.authors: Ashish Vaswani, Noam Shazeer, Niki Parmar, et al.
sources.note: Introduced the Transformer. The single most consequential architecture paper of the 2010s, underpinning GPT, BERT, and virtually all foundation models.

sources.title: Induction of Pluripotent Stem Cells from Mouse Embryonic and Adult Fibroblast Cultures by Defined Factors
sources.authors: Kazutoshi Takahashi, Shinya Yamanaka
sources.note: Discovered that mature cells can be reprogrammed to pluripotency (iPSC). (Gurdon, Yamanaka: Nobel Medicine 2012)

sources.title: The Sequence of the Human Genome
sources.authors: J. Craig Venter, Mark D. Adams, Eugene W. Myers, et al.
sources.note: First draft sequence of the human genome. One of the most consequential infrastructure projects in biology.

sources.title: Helical microtubules of graphitic carbon
sources.authors: Sumio Iijima
sources.note: Discovery of carbon nanotubes. Opened an entire field of nanomaterials science and engineering.

sources.title: A method for obtaining digital signatures and public-key cryptosystems
sources.authors: R. L. Rivest, A. Shamir, L. Adleman
sources.note: Invented the RSA public-key cryptosystem. The foundation of internet security, digital signatures, and secure communication.

sources.title: Backpropagation Applied to Handwritten Zip Code Recognition
sources.authors: Yann LeCun, B. Boser, J. S. Denker, et al.
sources.note: The first practical CNN paper. Applied backpropagation to convolutional networks for handwritten digit recognition — the origin of deep computer vision.

sources.title: Highly accurate protein structure prediction with AlphaFold
sources.authors: John Jumper, K Taki, Alexander Pritzel, Tim Green, Michael Figurnov, Olaf Ronneberger, et al.
sources.note: AlphaFold2. Solved the 50-year protein folding problem. Hassabis and Jumper: Nobel Chemistry 2024.

sources.title: Gradient-based learning applied to document recognition
sources.authors: Yann LeCun, Léon Bottou, Yoshua Bengio, Patrick Haffner
sources.note: Foundational CNN architecture paper. Introduced LeNet and the convolutional-pooling-dense pipeline that all modern vision models descend from.

sources.title: A programmable dual-RNA-guided DNA endonuclease in adaptive bacterial immunity
sources.authors: Martin Jinek, Krzysztof Chylinski, Ines Fonfara, et al.
sources.note: Demonstrated programmable genome editing with CRISPR-Cas9. Doudna and Charpentier: Nobel Chemistry 2020.

sources.title: Candela-class high-brightness InGaN/AlGaN double-heterostructure blue-light-emitting diodes
sources.authors: Shuji Nakamura, Takashi Mukai, Masayuki Senoh
sources.note: First high-brightness blue LED enabling white LED lighting. (Akasaki, Amano, Nakamura: Nobel Physics 2014)

sources.title: Synthesis of LiCoO₂ from spent lithium-ion batteries
sources.authors: M. Stanley Whittingham
sources.note: Foundational work on lithium-ion battery cathode materials. (Goodenough, Whittingham, Yoshino: Nobel Chemistry 2019)

sources.title: Enhancement of Antitumor Immunity by CTLA-4 Blockade
sources.authors: Dana R. Leach, Matthew F. Krummel, James P. Allison
sources.note: Demonstrated CTLA-4 blockade for cancer immunotherapy. (Allison, Honjo: Nobel Medicine 2018)

sources.title: Bitcoin: A Peer-to-Peer Electronic Cash System
sources.authors: Satoshi Nakamoto
sources.note: Invented blockchain and proof-of-work consensus for decentralized digital currency. Spawned an entire technology sector.

sources.title: Polynomial-Time Algorithms for Prime Factorization and Discrete Logarithms on a Quantum Computer
sources.authors: Peter W. Shor
sources.note: Demonstrated exponential quantum speedup for factoring. The paper that made quantum computing a practical engineering goal.

sources.title: Observation of Bose-Einstein Condensation in a Dilute Atomic Vapor
sources.authors: M. H. Anderson, J. R. Ensher, M. R. Matthews, C. E. Wieman, E. A. Cornell
sources.note: First observation of Bose-Einstein condensation in dilute gas. (Cornell, Ketterle, Wieman: Nobel Physics 2001)

sources.title: Evidence for oscillation of atmospheric neutrinos
sources.authors: Super-Kamiokande Collaboration
sources.note: Discovery of neutrino oscillations proving neutrinos have mass. (Kajita, McDonald: Nobel Physics 2015)

sources.title: Achievement of Target Gain Larger than Unity in an Inertial Fusion Experiment
sources.authors: H. Abu-Shawareb, R. Acree, P. Adams, et al.
sources.note: First demonstration of fusion ignition with target gain >1 at the National Ignition Facility. A milestone for fusion energy.

sources.title: Long Short-Term Memory
sources.authors: Sepp Hochreiter, Jürgen Schmidhuber
sources.note: Invented the LSTM gated recurrent cell, solving the vanishing gradient problem and enabling modern sequence modelling.

sources.title: Deep learning
sources.authors: Yann LeCun, Yoshua Bengio, Geoffrey Hinton
sources.note: The canonical deep learning review by the three pioneers (LeCun, Bengio, Hinton). Hinton and Hopfield: Nobel Physics 2024 for foundational ML discoveries.

sources.title: Observation of Gravitational Waves from a Binary Black Hole Merger
sources.authors: LIGO Scientific Collaboration and Virgo Collaboration
sources.note: First direct detection of gravitational waves by LIGO. Weiss, Barish, Thorne: Nobel Physics 2017.

sources.title: Attosecond metrology
sources.authors: M. Hentschel, R. Kienberger, Ch. Spielmann, et al.
sources.note: First isolated attosecond light pulses, opening real-time observation of electron dynamics. (Agostini, Krausz, L'Huillier: Nobel Physics 2023)

sources.title: Electron counting and beam-induced motion correction enable near-atomic-resolution single-particle cryo-EM
sources.authors: Xueming Li, Paul Mooney, Shawn Zheng, et al.
sources.note: Key advance enabling near-atomic resolution cryo-EM. (Dubochet, Frank, Henderson: Nobel Chemistry 2017)

sources.title: Click Chemistry: Diverse Chemical Function from a Few Good Reactions
sources.authors: Hartmuth C. Kolb, M. G. Finn, K. Barry Sharpless
sources.note: Defined click chemistry — reliable modular reactions for molecular assembly. (Bertozzi, Meldal, Sharpless: Nobel Chemistry 2022)

sources.title: Millisecond-timescale, genetically targeted optical control of neural activity
sources.authors: Edward S. Boyden, Feng Zhang, Ernst Bamberg, et al.
sources.note: Launched optogenetics — genetically targeted optical control of neurons with millisecond precision.

sources.title: Robust Uncertainty Principles: Exact Signal Reconstruction from Highly Incomplete Frequency Information
sources.authors: Emmanuel J. Candès, Justin Romberg, Terence Tao
sources.note: Founded compressed sensing theory — exact reconstruction from far fewer measurements than Nyquist. Transformed MRI, radar, and signal processing.

sources.title: A new polynomial-time algorithm for linear programming
sources.authors: Narendra Karmarkar
sources.note: Introduced interior-point methods for linear programming. Revolutionized large-scale optimization across engineering and operations research.

sources.title: Reconstituting Organ-Level Lung Functions on a Chip
sources.authors: Dongeun Huh, Benjamin D. Matthews, Akiko Mammoto, Martin Montoya‐Zavala, Hong Yuan Hsin, Donald E. Ingber
sources.note: First lung-on-a-chip recreating organ-level functions. Launched the organ-on-chip field for drug testing and disease modelling.

sources.title: The James Webb Space Telescope Mission
sources.venue: Publications of the Astronomical Society of the Pacific
sources.authors: Jonathan P. Gardner, John C. Mather, R. Abbott, James S. Abell, Mark Abernathy, Faith Abney, et al.
sources.note: Overview of the JWST mission — the most powerful space telescope ever built, extending observability to the earliest galaxies.

sources.title: The anatomy of a large-scale hypertextual Web search engine
sources.authors: Sergey Brin, Lawrence Page
sources.note: Introduced PageRank and the architecture of Google. Defined web-scale information retrieval and graph-based ranking.

sources.title: ImageNet Classification with Deep Convolutional Neural Networks
sources.authors: Alex Krizhevsky, Ilya Sutskever, Geoffrey E. Hinton
sources.note: AlexNet. Won ILSVRC 2012 by a huge margin using GPU-trained deep CNNs, triggering the modern deep learning era. (Hinton: Nobel Physics 2024)

sources.title: Electric Field Effect in Atomically Thin Carbon Films
sources.authors: K. S. Novoselov, A. K. Geim, S. V. Morozov, et al.
sources.note: First isolation and characterisation of graphene. Novoselov and Geim: Nobel Physics 2010.

sources.title: Quantum Spin Hall Effect and Topological Phase Transition in HgTe Quantum Wells
sources.authors: B. Andrei Bernevig, Taylor L. Hughes, Shou-Cheng Zhang
sources.note: Predicted the quantum spin Hall effect in HgTe, confirmed experimentally. Opened the field of topological insulators. (Kosterlitz, Thouless, Haldane: Nobel Physics 2016)

sources.title: Directed evolution of subtilisin E in Bacillus subtilis to enhance total activity in aqueous dimethylformamide
sources.venue: Annals of the New York Academy of Sciences
sources.authors: Keqin Chen, Frances H. Arnold
sources.note: Pioneered laboratory directed evolution of enzymes. (Frances Arnold: Nobel Chemistry 2018)

sources.title: Isolation of a cDNA clone derived from a blood-borne non-A, non-B viral hepatitis genome
sources.authors: Qui-Lim Choo, George Kuo, Amy J. Weiner, et al.
sources.note: Discovery of the Hepatitis C virus. (Alter, Houghton, Rice: Nobel Medicine 2020)

sources.title: Chimeric Antigen Receptor–Modified T Cells in Chronic Lymphoid Leukemia
sources.authors: David L. Porter, Bruce L. Levine, Michael Kalos, et al.
sources.note: First clinical demonstration of CAR-T cell therapy achieving complete remission in leukemia patients.

sources.title: Experimental quantum teleportation
sources.authors: Dik Bouwmeester, Jian-Wei Pan, Klaus Mattle, et al.
sources.note: First experimental realization of quantum teleportation. Foundational for quantum communication and quantum networks.

sources.title: Efficient Hybrid Solar Cells Based on Meso-Superstructured Organometal Halide Perovskites
sources.authors: Michael M. Lee, Joël Teuscher, Tsutomu Miyasaka, et al.
sources.note: Demonstrated high-efficiency perovskite solar cells. Triggered the fastest efficiency ramp in photovoltaic history.

sources.title: Neuronal ensemble control of prosthetic devices by a human with tetraplegia
sources.authors: Leigh R. Hochberg, Mijail D. Serruya, et al.
sources.note: First demonstration of a human controlling a prosthetic via intracortical brain-computer interface (BrainGate).

sources.title: Support-vector networks
sources.authors: Corinna Cortes, Vladimir Vapnik
sources.note: Introduced the support vector machine, the dominant classifier before deep learning and still widely used in science and engineering.

sources.title: Generative Adversarial Nets
sources.authors: Ian J. Goodfellow, Jean Pouget-Abadie, Mehdi Mirza, et al.
sources.note: Introduced the GAN framework for implicit generative modelling. Opened entirely new directions in image synthesis, domain adaptation, and data augmentation.

sources.title: The Google File System
sources.authors: Sanjay Ghemawat, Howard Gobioff, Shun-Tak Leung
sources.note: Defined the scalable distributed file system architecture. Inspired HDFS and all modern cloud storage systems.

sources.title: Surface codes: Towards practical large-scale quantum computation
sources.authors: Austin G. Fowler, Matteo Mariantoni, John M. Martinis, Andrew N. Cleland
sources.note: Established the surface code as the leading architecture for fault-tolerant quantum computing.

sources.title: Unidirectional Rotation in a Mechanically Interlocked Molecular Rotor
sources.authors: David A. Leigh, Jenny K. Y. Wong, François Dehez, Fabien Zerbetto
sources.note: Demonstrated unidirectional molecular rotation. (Sauvage, Stoddart, Feringa: Nobel Chemistry 2016)

sources.title: Active pixel sensor array with electronic shuttering
sources.authors: Eric R. Fossum
sources.note: Pioneered the CMOS active-pixel sensor (APS). Replaced CCD in virtually all cameras, from smartphones to spacecraft.

sources.title: Random Forests
sources.authors: Leo Breiman
sources.note: Introduced the random forest ensemble method. One of the most widely deployed ML algorithms across all of science and industry.

sources.title: MapReduce: Simplified Data Processing on Large Clusters
sources.authors: Jeffrey Dean, Sanjay Ghemawat
sources.note: Introduced MapReduce. Defined the programming model for warehouse-scale batch data processing and launched the big data ecosystem.

sources.title: Dynamo: Amazon's Highly Available Key-value Store
sources.authors: Giuseppe DeCandia, Deniz Hastorun, Madan Jampani, et al.
sources.note: Defined eventually consistent key-value stores. Inspired Cassandra, Riak, and DynamoDB — the NoSQL movement.

sources.title: ImageNet: A Large-Scale Hierarchical Image Database
sources.authors: Jia Deng, Wei Dong, Richard Socher, Li-Jia Li, Kai Li, Li Fei-Fei
sources.note: Created the ImageNet benchmark that catalysed the deep learning revolution. The annual ILSVRC competition reshaped computer vision.

sources.title: Efficient Estimation of Word Representations in Vector Space
sources.authors: Tomas Mikolov, Kai Chen, Greg Corrado, Jeffrey Dean
sources.note: Introduced Word2Vec. Demonstrated that distributed word representations encode semantic relationships, launching the era of pre-trained embeddings.

sources.title: Language Models are Few-Shot Learners
sources.authors: Tom B. Brown, Benjamin Mann, Nick Ryder, et al.
sources.note: GPT-3. Demonstrated that scaling language models to 175B parameters unlocks in-context learning and few-shot capabilities across tasks.

sources.title: mRNA vaccines — a new era in vaccinology
sources.authors: Norbert Pardi, Michael J. Hogan, Frederick Porter, Drew Weissman
sources.note: Definitive review of the mRNA vaccine platform. Karikó and Weissman: Nobel Physiology or Medicine 2023 for nucleoside-modified mRNA.

sources.title: Mastering the Game of Go with Deep Neural Networks and Tree Search
sources.authors: David Silver, Aja Huang, Chris J. Maddison, et al.
sources.note: AlphaGo. Defeated the world champion at Go using deep RL + Monte Carlo tree search, proving AI could master complex strategic domains.

sources.title: Dropout: A Simple Way to Prevent Neural Networks from Overfitting
sources.authors: Nitish Srivastava, Geoffrey Hinton, Alex Krizhevsky, et al.
sources.note: Introduced dropout regularization. One of the most widely used techniques for preventing overfitting in deep networks.

sources.title: BERT: Pre-training of Deep Bidirectional Transformers for Language Understanding
sources.authors: Jacob Devlin, Ming-Wei Chang, Kenton Lee, Kristina Toutanova
matched_topics: bert pre-training of deep bidirectional transformers
sources.note: Established masked-language pretraining as the dominant NLP paradigm and showed that deep bidirectional context dramatically improves all downstream tasks.

sources.title: Adam: A Method for Stochastic Optimization
sources.authors: Diederik P. Kingma, Jimmy Ba
sources.note: Introduced the Adam optimizer, combining adaptive learning rates with momentum. The most widely used optimizer in deep learning.

sources.title: Denoising Diffusion Probabilistic Models
sources.authors: Jonathan Ho, Ajay Jain, Pieter Abbeel
sources.note: Revived diffusion-based generative modelling and proved it can match GAN image quality. Underpins Stable Diffusion, DALL-E, and molecular generation.

sources.title: Human-level control through deep reinforcement learning
sources.authors: Volodymyr Mnih, Koray Kavukcuoglu, David Silver, et al.
sources.note: DQN. Demonstrated that deep neural networks can learn control policies directly from raw pixels at human level across Atari games.

sources.title: U-Net: Convolutional Networks for Biomedical Image Segmentation
sources.authors: Olaf Ronneberger, Philipp Fischer, Thomas Brox
sources.note: Introduced U-Net encoder-decoder with skip connections for semantic segmentation. Became the default architecture across biomedical imaging and beyond.

sources.title: An Image is Worth 16x16 Words: Transformers for Image Recognition at Scale
sources.authors: Alexey Dosovitskiy, Lucas Beyer, Alexander Kolesnikov, et al.
sources.note: Vision Transformer (ViT). Showed transformers can replace CNNs in vision when scaled with enough data, unifying vision and language architectures.

sources.title: Loophole-free Bell inequality violation using electron spins separated by 1.3 kilometres
sources.authors: B. Hensen, H. Bernien, A. E. Dréau, et al.
sources.note: First loophole-free Bell test, conclusively ruling out local hidden variables. Aspect, Clauser, Zeilinger: Nobel Physics 2022.

sources.title: Batch Normalization: Accelerating Deep Network Training by Reducing Internal Covariate Shift
sources.authors: Sergey Ioffe, Christian Szegedy
sources.note: Introduced batch normalization, enabling much deeper and faster-converging networks. A standard component of virtually every modern deep architecture.

sources.title: XGBoost: A Scalable Tree Boosting System
sources.authors: Tianqi Chen, Carlos Guestrin
sources.note: XGBoost. The dominant tabular ML system. Won nearly every Kaggle competition for years and remains the standard for structured data in industry.

sources.title: Semi-Supervised Classification with Graph Convolutional Networks
sources.authors: Thomas N. Kipf, Max Welling
sources.note: Introduced the spectral graph convolution framework (GCN). Foundational for all graph neural network research in chemistry, materials, social networks, etc.

sources.title: OpenFlow: Enabling Innovation in Campus Networks
sources.authors: Nick McKeown, Tom Anderson, Hari Balakrishnan, et al.
sources.note: Launched software-defined networking (SDN) by separating the control plane from forwarding, enabling programmable network infrastructure.

sources.title: NeRF: Representing Scenes as Neural Radiance Fields for View Synthesis
sources.authors: Ben Mildenhall, Pratul P. Srinivasan, Matthew Tancik, et al.
sources.note: Introduced Neural Radiance Fields for photorealistic novel view synthesis. Launched an entire subfield of neural scene representation.

sources.title: The missing memristor found
sources.authors: Dmitri B. Strukov, Gregory S. Snider, Duncan R. Stewart, R. Stanley Williams
sources.note: First demonstration of the memristor, the fourth fundamental circuit element. Opened neuromorphic computing and non-volatile memory directions.

sources.title: Spanner: Google's globally-distributed database
sources.authors: James C. Corbett, Jeffrey Dean, Michael Epstein, et al.
sources.note: First globally-distributed database with external consistency. Proved that strong consistency at global scale is achievable.

sources.title: Google Earth Engine: Planetary-scale geospatial analysis for everyone
sources.authors: Noel Gorelick, M. Hancher, Mike Dixon, Simon Ilyushchenko, David Thau, Rebecca Moore
sources.note: Made planetary-scale geospatial analysis accessible to non-specialists via cloud computation over petabytes of satellite imagery.

sources.title: Large-scale cluster management at Google with Borg
sources.venue: Proceedings of the Tenth European Conference on Computer Systems
sources.authors: Abhishek Verma, Luis Pedrosa, Madhukar Korupolu, David Oppenheimer, Eric Tune, John Wilkes
sources.note: Described Google's Borg cluster manager, the operational ancestor of Kubernetes. Defined warehouse-scale resource scheduling.

sources.title: In Search of an Understandable Consensus Algorithm
sources.authors: Diego Ongaro, John Ousterhout
sources.note: Introduced the Raft consensus algorithm. Now the most widely deployed consensus protocol (etcd, CockroachDB, TiKV).

sources.title: The Digital Twin: Realizing the Cyber-Physical Production System for Industry 4.0
sources.authors: Michael Grieves, John Vickers
sources.note: Defined the digital-twin concept as a production-scale cyber-physical control surface for Industry 4.0.

sources.title: Compressed sensing
sources.authors: David L. Donoho
sources.note: Selected for strong match to compressed sensing; tagged as Cross-domain transfer, Representation and modeling shift in AI and compute.

sources.authors: David Huang, Eric A. Swanson, Charles P. Lin, Joel S. Schuman, William G. Stinson, Warren Chang, et al.
sources.note: Selected for strong match to optical coherence tomography; tagged as Cross-domain transfer, New instrument or platform, New observability in Photonics, imaging, and sensing.

sources.note: Selected for strong match to openflow; tagged as Shared infrastructure and protocols, Search, optimization, and control in Systems and networks.

sources.authors: A. K. Geǐm, Kostya S. Novoselov
sources.note: Selected for strong match to graphene; tagged as Cross-domain transfer, New substrate, material, or device in Materials, chemistry, and energy.

sources.authors: Christian L. Degen, Friedemann Reinhard, Paola Cappellaro

sources.venue: 2014 IEEE Conference on Computer Vision and Pattern Recognition
sources.authors: Ross Girshick, Jeff Donahue, Trevor Darrell, Jitendra Malik
sources.note: Selected for strong match to semantic segmentation; tagged as Cross-domain transfer, Representation and modeling shift in AI and compute.

sources.authors: Mohammad Al-Fares, Alexander Loukissas, Amin Vahdat

sources.note: Selected for strong match to a scalable commodity data center network architecture; tagged as Shared infrastructure and protocols, Scaling architecture in Systems and networks.

sources.authors: A. H. Castro Neto, F. Guinea, N. M. R. Peres, Kostya S. Novoselov, A. K. Geǐm
sources.note: Selected for strong match to graphene; tagged as Cross-domain transfer, New substrate, material, or device in Materials, chemistry, and energy.

sources.venue: 2022 IEEE/CVF Conference on Computer Vision and Pattern Recognition (CVPR)
sources.authors: Robin Rombach, Andreas Blattmann, Dominik Lorenz, Patrick Esser, Björn Ommer
sources.note: Selected for strong match to latent diffusion models; tagged as Cross-domain transfer, Representation and modeling shift in AI and compute.

sources.venue: 2017 IEEE Conference on Computer Vision and Pattern Recognition (CVPR)
sources.authors: Christian Ledig, Lucas Theis, Ferenc Huszár, José Caballero, Andrew Cunningham, Alejandro Acosta, et al.
sources.note: Selected for strong match to generative adversarial network; tagged as Cross-domain transfer, Representation and modeling shift in AI and compute.
